title: Bucie is back with record label and new single
bodyText:
ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - Singer and businesswoman Bucie Nkomo (Nqwiliso) is counted among ‘comeback kids’ in the music industry with her latest single Faithful.


> In her exciting music career, with hits such as Easy to Love, the songbird revived her working chemistry with her long-time producer, Demor Sikhosana, to release the new single.


The Shela singer described her music break, since 2016, as a time to focus on building her new record label, Bucie Pty Ltd.

Bucie speaks of her sound as unique, with a sense of nostalgia. In an interview with TshisaLive about latest single, she said: “We are staying true to our music identities while also incorporating current sounds. This blend creates a unique and memorable experience for our listeners. You can expect a sense of nostalgia as Demor and I have always had a great working chemistry. I’m grateful to everyone who has listened to Faithful and for the positive sentiments I’ve been receiving,” said Bucie.

Speaking to the publication about her new record label, Bucie said: “Music is my life, so it takes up most of my time. However, I’ve also been focusing on my record label, Bucie Pty Ltd, and planning to grow my business to support emerging artists. Additionally, I enjoy spending quality time with my family and exploring new creative ventures.


> “Throughout my journey, God has always been at the centre of how I’ve moved… I continue to pay Him honour for the gift of my voice and the healing it brings through music. As I navigate the path ahead, I trust in his guidance and remain grateful for the opportunities that lie ahead,” she said.


The post Bucie speaks about starting her record label and comeback in the industry appeared first on Bona Magazine.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
